name: Humanes to Tortuero and the Val
name_original: Tortuero y Puente Romano de Valdesotos — circular desde Humanes
description: This difficult 46.5-mile (74.8 km) road cycling route from Humanes explores the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara.
region_intro: Road cycling around Humanes offers diverse terrain, from gentle routes across river plains to challenging ascents in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including riparian forests, open fields, and areas leading towards more mountainous features. This provides cyclists with a range of options suitable for different skill levels and preferences. Best road cycling routes around Humanes The most popular road cycling route is Beleña Reservoir and Tamajón, a 37.7 miles (60.7 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 57 minutes to complete. This route offers varied scenery around the reservoir and through the village. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Humanes to Tortuero and the Val, a difficult 46.5 miles (74.8 km) path. This route traverses varied terrain with significant elevation gain, connecting different towns and open landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the From Malaguilla to the medieval village of Hita, a 47.7 miles (76.8 km) trail leading through rural areas and towards a historic village, often completed in about 3 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Humanes is defined by river plains, foothills leading to the Sierra de Guadarrama, and varied open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in Humanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Humanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Humanes?
A: There are nearly 50 road cycling routes around Humanes, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 800 times, providing an average rating of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Humanes?
A: Road cycling around Humanes features diverse terrain. You can find gentle routes across river plains along the Tajo, Tajuña, and Jarama rivers, offering scenic views of riparian forests. For more challenging rides, routes extend into the foothills leading to the Sierra de Guadarrama, providing significant elevation gains and rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Humanes?
A: Yes, Humanes offers a few easier road cycling routes. While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are options that cater to beginners, particularly those traversing the flatter river plains. These routes provide a good introduction to the region's cycling without excessive climbs.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Experienced cyclists looking for a challenge can explore routes with significant elevation gain. A notable difficult route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016700000/humanes-to-tortuero-and-the-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humanes to Tortuero and the Val&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 46.5 miles (74.8 km) and features varied terrain. The region also connects to the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, offering climbs like the Mortirolo Climb of Batres, which has an average gradient of four percent over two kilometers.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by interesting landmarks and natural features. You can cycle towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;medieval village of Hita&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanar Public Washhouse&lt;/a&gt;, or cross the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4624145" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Bridge over the Henares River&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts river plains, riparian forests, and views towards the Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling around Humanes?
A: Yes, there are several loop routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40331069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tórtola de Henares – Fontanar Public Washhouse loop from Humanes de Mohernando&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 45.5 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016655020/belena-reservoir-and-tamajon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beleña Reservoir and Tamajón&lt;/a&gt; loop, which is a moderate 60.7 km trail offering varied scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Humanes?
A: The diverse landscapes around Humanes make it suitable for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the river plains and foothills. While specific winter conditions are not detailed, the region's climate typically allows for cycling outside of extreme weather events.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options or networks connected to Humanes?
A: Yes, Humanes de Madrid benefits from its connection to the extensive &lt;strong&gt;CiclaMadrid network&lt;/strong&gt; and various &lt;strong&gt;Vías Verdes (Greenways)&lt;/strong&gt;. These networks, often converted from disused railway lines, provide extensive options for longer rides and exploring the natural environment beyond the immediate vicinity of Humanes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Humanes?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Humanes, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which offers everything from gentle river plain rides to challenging ascents in the Sierra de Guadarrama foothills, as well as the scenic beauty of the varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through historical or cultural sites?
A: Absolutely. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016668970/from-malaguilla-to-the-medieval-village-of-hita" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Malaguilla to the medieval village of Hita&lt;/a&gt; lead directly to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2104134" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;village of Hita&lt;/a&gt;, known for its rich history. The area also features other historical points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6322347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Hita&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687203" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanar Public Washhouse&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several routes offer rewarding views. For instance, the foothills leading to the Sierra de Guadarrama provide climbs with rewarding vistas. You can also find specific viewpoints such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5410859" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cogolludo&lt;/a&gt;, which can be incorporated into your cycling itinerary for panoramic scenery.

Q: What is the average distance and duration for road cycling routes in Humanes?
A: The routes vary significantly in distance and duration. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016655020/belena-reservoir-and-tamajon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beleña Reservoir and Tamajón&lt;/a&gt; is about 60.7 km (37.7 miles) and takes around 2 hours 57 minutes. More challenging routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016700000/humanes-to-tortuero-and-the-val" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Humanes to Tortuero and the Val&lt;/a&gt;, cover 74.8 km (46.5 miles) and can take over 3.5 hours.
tips: The Roman Bridge of Valdesotos, located near the hamlet of the same name in the Sierra Norte of Guadalajara, is actually of medieval origin, probably from the 12th century, with main use during the Middle Ages and converted as a passage to the Monastery of Bonaval.
Small town located at the extreme north of the province of Guadalajara, at the first mountains of the Sierra de Guadarrama!

It is located within the Campiña de Guadalajara, at the foot of the foothills of the Serranía. Its term includes three small plateaus that are the places of the Muela, the Chaparral and the Llano.
In addition, Matarrubia has a main water course that passes through the urban area, the so-called "Matarrubia stream", a tributary of the Jarama.

faqs.question: How long does this road cycling route typically take?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route, covering approximately 74.8 kilometers with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain, is estimated to take around 3 hours and 40 minutes to complete for an experienced racebike cyclist. Your actual duration may vary based on your fitness level and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as difficult. Cyclists should be prepared for significant elevation changes and potentially variable road conditions. While some sections may be smooth, others, particularly service roads like parts of the "Camino de servicio del canal de Isabel II" between Tortuero and Valdesotos, can be quite bumpy and require caution.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route from Humanes?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, Humanes (likely Humanes de Mohernando) is the suggested starting point. You would typically look for public parking within the village. As it's a circular route, you will return to your starting point.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful views of the Guadalajara province, known for its natural beauty, open landscapes, and quiet roads. You can expect to see gentle hills, potentially pass through lavender fields and endless meadows, and experience the picturesque natural environments characteristic of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights along the route include the historic Roman Bridge of Valdesotos and the charming Tortuero Stone Bridge. You will also pass through the settlement of Matarrubia, offering a glimpse into rural Spanish life.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and road condition like for road cycling?
faqs.answer: The route primarily uses minor paved roads. However, cyclists should be aware that some sections, particularly service roads, can be quite bumpy. While many roads are quiet with little traffic, the overall quality of the pavement can vary, requiring attention and suitable tires for a racebike.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes on roads, which generally isn't suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the pavement for such a long distance. If you plan to bring a dog, it would typically need to be in a specialized trailer or carrier, and you should consider the dog's comfort and safety on a long, difficult road cycling route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Guadalajara region, including the Sierra Norte, is generally pleasant for cycling in spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder conditions of winter. Spring brings blooming landscapes, while autumn provides comfortable riding weather and beautiful foliage.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route, especially within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara?
faqs.answer: As this route is primarily on public roads and passes through the general area of the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas, and stay on designated paths or roads.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other notable trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You may encounter short sections of trails such as "De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa desde Tortuero a Torrelaguna)", "Camino de las Vargas al Molino", "Senda del Desfiladero de La Horca", and "Camino Alto de Retiendas a Valdesotos", among others. These intersections are generally minor portions of the overall road cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options along the route or in nearby villages?
faqs.answer: The villages along the route, such as Tortuero, Valdesotos, and Humanes, offer a glimpse into rural Spanish life. While not bustling urban centers, you may find small local cafes or restaurants in these settlements. For more extensive options or accommodation, it's advisable to check in advance, especially in the larger starting village of Humanes de Mohernando.
